preview_text:
The statistical model rates Turkey a narrow favourite at 37.5% to win, with the USA at 31.1%. The underdog edge is modest—a gap of 6 percentage points—suggesting competitive teams rather than a clear hierarchy. A draw accounts for 31.4% of the model's probability mass, indicating genuine uncertainty about which team prevails.

The win-draw-loss spread reveals a tightly balanced matchup. Turkey holds the slimmest advantage over the USA, while the draw probability sits nearly level with Turkey's outright win chance. This distribution points to a competitive encounter where all three outcomes remain plausible, with no single result commanding majority probability.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully. Bookmakers price the USA substantially higher at 46.3% (a gap of 15 percentage points above the model) and compress the draw to 23.7%, while shifting Turkey down to 29.9%. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the books. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct—only that the gap between the statistical model and market consensus is substantial enough to warrant attention from users.
